free_text: Cross sections of deuteron induced reactions on natCr
up to 50 MeV: Experiments and comparison with
theoretical codes
The Cr-targets were prepared by
electro deposition on 0.02375 mm thick, high purity Cu
backing. The actual thickness of the 32 individual
Cr-targets was derived from separate weighing and
accurate determination of the area (by scanning of
the individual irradiated targets to determine the
exact linear dimensions). The variation of target
thickness was  between 17.8 and 24.3 microns.
For the 50 MeV
irradiation at LLN the targets were placed in a
custom made Faraday cup like, air cooled target
holder with a long collimator, assuring that all
incoming charged particles hit the targets. The beam
current was kept constant at 110 nA during the 70 min
irradiation. No charge integration was available for
this irradiation. The 20 MeV deuteron beam
irradiation at VUB was performed using our standard
water cooled target holder with secondary electron
suppression and long collimator. Irradiation took
place with a constant 140 nA beam current for 60 min.
Total charge on target was collected by an
integrator. In order to get a good agreement of the
measured and recommended excitation functions the
incident energy for he VUB irradiation had to be
changed from 20.5 to 20.7 MeV and the current was
increased by 28%, while for the LLN beam the current
was decreased by 8% and no energy change was needed.
Chapter 4
Uncertainty on the energy scale includes
cumulative  effects of initial beam energy, stopping
estimation influenced by  target thickness and beam
straggling. Throughout the stack it increases  from
0.2 MeV for the first foil to 1.2 MeV at the last foil.
Estimation of the uncertainties on the cross
section values are  based on the standard approach as
the square root of the quadratic summation of  the
independently contributing parameters:
surface density of target nuclei (5%),
incident charged particle flux (5%),
energy dependent detector  efficiency (5%),
nuclear decay and branching data (5%).
statistical uncertainty  (2-8%),
This corresponds to an overall uncertainty of 10-13%.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes
ALICE-D, EMPIRE-D and TALYS.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
calculation of thick target yields.
